heritage.title: Five thousand years, in one drop
heritage.body: Sangam means confluence — the meeting of sacred rivers. Sangam Herbals is the meeting of ancient Ayurvedic wisdom and careful, modern production. Founded under the direction of Dr. Praveen Rathi, our formulations are made from authentic herbs, cold-pressed oils and time-honoured recipes, now prepared and clearly labelled for households around the world.
heritage.pillar1_b: Classical churnas, tablets and oils prepared to traditional Ayurvedic texts.
heritage.pillar2_b: Real ingredients, honest quality, no needless additives.
heritage.pillar3_b: Sourced at origin and delivered worldwide.

river.quote: Where two rivers become one remedy.
river.sub: Sangam is the meeting of sacred waters — and of ancient practice with careful, modern craft. Discover the attar library: 32 long-lasting Indian oil perfumes.

edu.learn_p1: Ayurveda is a traditional system of natural wellbeing that began in India around 5,000 years ago. Its name comes from two Sanskrit words: ayur, meaning life, and veda, meaning knowledge. At its heart is one simple idea: wellbeing comes from keeping the body and mind in balance.
edu.learn_p2: Rather than focusing on quick fixes, Ayurveda looks at the whole person and the rhythm of everyday life. It draws on plants, herbs and traditional formulations, alongside simple daily routines for diet, sleep and self-care. Think of it as a time-honoured approach to looking after yourself, gently and consistently.
edu.learn_pr1_t: Ayurveda sees wellbeing as a state of balance, and aims to keep the body and mind steady through the seasons and the demands of daily life.
edu.learn_pr2_t: It considers the person as a whole, taking in diet, sleep, activity and mood rather than looking at any one part in isolation.
edu.learn_pr3_t: Its preparations are built from plants, herbs, oils and other natural ingredients, made using traditional methods passed down over generations.
edu.learn_pr4_t: Much of Ayurveda lives in small, repeated daily habits — the simple acts of care that, over time, support a sense of vitality and everyday wellbeing.
edu.dosha_intro: In Ayurveda, three energies known as doshas — Vata, Pitta and Kapha — are present in everyone in a unique blend, and the aim is simply to keep your own mix in balance.
edu.vata_traits: Vata types tend to be light, quick and creative — lively in mind and body when in balance.
edu.vata_care: In Ayurveda, Vata is traditionally kept steady with warmth, routine and grounding, nourishing foods and oils.
edu.pitta_traits: Pitta types tend to be focused, driven and warm, with sharp energy and a strong sense of purpose.
edu.pitta_care: In Ayurveda, Pitta is traditionally kept balanced with cooling foods, calm routines and time spent in nature.
edu.kapha_traits: Kapha types tend to be calm, steady and grounded, with a settled, easy-going nature.
edu.kapha_care: In Ayurveda, Kapha is traditionally kept lively with movement, warming spices and energising daily rituals.
edu.about_title: A custodian of authentic Ayurveda, made at the source
edu.about_p1: Sangam Herbals is a family-run Ayurvedic house in India, founded under the direction of Dr. Praveen Rathi. Each formulation is prepared to classical Ayurvedic texts, using real herbs and cold-pressed oils rather than shortcuts or synthetic fillers. The recipes are old; the standards we hold them to are not.
edu.about_p2: Everything is made in India, at the source of the plants and the tradition itself. From there, our products are openly labelled and delivered worldwide. We believe the best way to earn your trust is to be plain about what we make and how we make it — and to let the products speak for themselves.
edu.about_pr1_t: Recipes are prepared to classical Ayurvedic texts, the way they have been made for generations.
edu.about_pr2_t: We work with genuine herbs and cold-pressed oils, with no synthetic fillers added for convenience.
edu.about_pr3_l: Made in India, at the source
edu.about_pr3_t: Every product is crafted in India, where the plants grow and the tradition began.
edu.about_pr4_t: Our range is clearly labelled, with ingredients and usage shown openly, and shipped worldwide.
edu.concern_intro: Not sure where to begin? Choose an everyday area you care about, and we'll point you to the products and traditions that fit.
edu.c_digestion_d: Comfortable digestion and everyday gut balance — classical churnas, carminative spices and gentle blends like Triphala and Avipattikar.
edu.c_immunity_d: Time-honoured tonics — chyawanprash, Amla, Tulsi and Giloy — traditionally valued in Ayurveda for everyday resilience.
edu.c_energy_d: Rejuvenating rasayana herbs and tonics — Shilajit, Shatavari, Bala and classic strength formulas — for everyday stamina.
edu.c_respiratory_d: Throat, chest and seasonal comfort, from Sitopaladi and Mulethi to soothing herbal lozenges.
edu.c_stress_d: Calming, grounding blends for focus and restful evenings — Brahmi, Shankhpushpi, Jatamansi and valerian.
edu.c_joints_d: Support for active bodies and easy movement — Guggul formulas and warming massage oils like Mahanarayan.
edu.c_womens_d: Gentle, traditional support for women's everyday balance — Shatavari, Ashoka and classic women's tonics.
edu.c_skin_d: Plant-based face and body care plus purifying herbs — cleansers, toners, hydrolats, Neem, Turmeric and Manjistha.
edu.c_hair_d: Nourishing hair and scalp care — Bhringraj and Amla oils, herbal shampoos and natural henna colour.
edu.c_oral_d: Natural daily care for teeth, gums and fresh breath — herbal toothpastes and time-tested ingredients like Neem.
edu.c_food_d: The kitchen side of Ayurveda — authentic spices and masalas, herbal and Indian teas, juices, sweets and more.
edu.c_fragrance_d: Incense and long-lasting Indian attars to scent your space and everyday ritual.
edu.herb_intro: A plain-language guide to the eight herbs you will meet most often across our catalogue, and the role each one has long held in Ayurvedic tradition.
edu.herb_triphala_what: A classic blend of three dried fruits, ground together into a single powder.
edu.herb_triphala_trad: In Ayurveda, traditionally used to support everyday digestion and gentle internal balance.
edu.herb_tulsi_what: An aromatic leaf from the holy basil plant, used as a powder, in teas and in skin and hair care.
edu.herb_tulsi_trad: Long valued in Ayurvedic tradition to support everyday wellbeing and a sense of vitality.
edu.herb_neem_what: A bitter leaf from the neem tree, used as a powder and throughout cleansing skin and hair formulas.
edu.herb_neem_trad: Traditionally associated in Ayurveda with everyday skin care and a clean, balanced complexion.
edu.herb_amla_what: A small, sour green fruit, used as a powder, a juice and in hair-care products.
edu.herb_amla_trad: Traditionally valued in Ayurveda to support everyday vitality and the care of hair and scalp.
edu.herb_brahmi_what: A small creeping herb, taken as a powder or in tablet form.
edu.herb_brahmi_trad: In Ayurveda, traditionally associated with calm focus and everyday mental wellbeing.
edu.herb_chyawanprash_what: A traditional dark herbal jam built around amla and a blend of many herbs and spices.
edu.herb_chyawanprash_trad: Long taken in Ayurvedic tradition as a daily tonic to support everyday vitality and wellbeing.
edu.herb_turmeric_what: A golden-yellow root, ground into a warm spice powder and used in cooking, juices and skin care.
edu.herb_turmeric_trad: Traditionally valued in Ayurveda to support everyday balance and the care of the skin.
edu.herb_henna_what: A leaf milled into a fine green powder that releases a natural reddish colour.
edu.herb_henna_trad: Long used in Ayurvedic and Indian tradition as a natural colour and conditioning treatment for hair.
edu.step1_t: Spend a few minutes understanding what Ayurveda is — a traditional Indian system of everyday wellbeing, balance and self-care.
edu.step2_t: Browse by what you care about — skin, hair, digestion, daily routine — and see which traditions and ingredients fit you.
edu.step3_t: Choose one product that suits your routine, try it consistently, and add more only once it feels right.
edu.faq1_q: Is Ayurveda safe, and how do I use the products?
edu.faq1_a: Our products are made from traditional plant-based ingredients and come with clear directions for everyday use. As a sensible precaution, please consult your doctor before starting anything new if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, taking medication, or under medical care.
edu.faq2_q: How do I choose if I'm completely new to this?
edu.faq2_a: Start with a single product that matches one everyday concern — such as skin, hair or daily care — rather than buying several at once. Each product explains in plain language what it is, how it has traditionally been used, and how to apply it.
edu.faq3_q: Are the products authentic and properly labelled?
edu.faq3_a: Every product is made in India under the direction of Dr. Praveen Rathi, following traditional Ayurvedic preparation and applicable quality and labelling standards. Ingredients and usage are listed openly on each product so you always know what you are getting.
edu.faq4_q: Do you ship to my country?
edu.faq4_a: Yes — we ship internationally. Delivery times and any applicable charges or duties are shown clearly at checkout before you confirm your order.

pdp.caution: Traditional Ayurvedic information for education, not medical advice. If you are pregnant, breastfeeding, taking medication or under medical care, please consult your doctor before use.

We earn your trust the honest way: open ingredients, clear labelling, and a family house that answers for every batch.

pdp.compare_sub: What to look for in an authentic Ayurvedic range — and how we measure up.
pdp.faq5_q: How soon will I notice anything?
pdp.faq5_a: Ayurvedic herbs are traditionally taken as part of a steady daily routine rather than a quick fix, and many people make them a quiet part of their day over several weeks. Results vary from person to person.
pdp.faq6_q: Who is this best suited to — and who should take care?
pdp.faq6_a: It is traditionally used in Ayurveda to support everyday wellbeing. If you are pregnant, breastfeeding, taking medication or under medical care, please speak with your doctor before use.

trust.std_intro: We would rather show you how a product is made than tell you it is good — open ingredient lists, clear labelling, and the source it comes from. Every formulation is prepared to classical Ayurvedic texts, from real herbs and cold-pressed oils, with nothing added for convenience. The recipes are centuries old; the discipline we hold them to is exact.

std_items.label: Real herbs, cold-pressed oils
std_items.text: Genuine botanicals and oils pressed without heat to keep their character intact — the plant itself, worked the traditional way, never reduced to anonymous extract.

std_items.label: No synthetic fillers
std_items.text: We don't bulk our churnas, tablets and oils with synthetic fillers, dyes or needless additives. A formula contains what the recipe calls for — nothing more.

std_items.label: Prepared to classical methods
std_items.text: Each formulation follows the classical Ayurvedic texts — the same proportions and methods passed down over generations, made the way the tradition prescribes, batch after batch.

std_items.label: Ingredients listed openly
std_items.text: Every product carries its full ingredient list, format and quantity — nothing hidden behind a vague 'proprietary blend'. Read exactly what's inside before you buy, and again on the label.

std_items.label: Clearly labelled
std_items.text: Our range is clearly labelled, with ingredient, usage and storage information presented openly to high standards, and shipped worldwide.

std_items.label: Consistent, batch to batch
std_items.text: Because the inputs are real and the method is fixed, the product you reorder is the product you trusted the first time. Quality holds steady — not just on a good day.

std_items.label: Made in India, at the source
std_items.text: Made where the plants grow and the practice originated, under the direction of Dr. Praveen Rathi — a shorter distance between herb and formulation, and a family house answerable for every batch.

std_items.label: Plain about what we don't claim
std_items.text: We describe our products in traditional, educational terms and make no medical claims. What we promise is specific: authentic formulas, real ingredients, open labelling, responsive support.

faq.q: Who makes Sangam Herbals, and where?
faq.a: Sangam Herbals is a family Ayurvedic house, formulated and overseen by Dr. Praveen Rathi. Everything is made in India, at the source of the herbs, and prepared to classical Ayurvedic texts — then clearly labelled to high standards.

faq.q: How do I know what is actually in each product?
faq.a: Every product lists its full ingredients, format and quantity — on the product page before you buy, and on the label when it arrives. We use real herbs and cold-pressed oils, with no synthetic fillers and nothing hidden behind a vague 'proprietary blend'.
Yes. Our range is clearly labelled, with ingredient, usage and storage information presented openly to high standards, and we ship worldwide.

faq.q: What if something is wrong with my order, or I change my mind?
faq.a: If your order arrives damaged, incomplete or not as described, we'll make it right — replacement or refund, no debate. And you have 14 days from delivery to return unopened items for a refund, with clear instructions in every order.

trust.safety_note: For wellbeing and traditional care only — not a medicine, and not a substitute for professional medical advice. Read the label, follow the directions, and consult your doctor if you are pregnant, nursing, on medication, or managing a health condition. Patch-test before first topical use. Keep out of reach of children.

founder.heading: I answer for every batch we make.
paragraphs: Ayurveda was not handed to my family as a business. It was handed down as a responsibility — a way of preparing remedies exactly as the classical texts set out, from real herbs and cold-pressed oils, with nothing added for convenience. That is the only way I know how to make them, and the only way I will.

Everything we send out into the world is made here in India, at the source of the plants and the practice itself. I work to the same proportions and methods that have been followed for generations, and I hold each formulation to standards that are exact — because the recipe being old is no excuse for the making being careless.

I have chosen to put my name on this house, and on this page, for a simple reason: when something goes into your body or onto your skin, you deserve to know who stands behind it. Read the full ingredients before you buy. Write to us if anything is unclear. And if a product is not right for you, tell us, and we will make it right.
founder.credential: Dr. Praveen Rathi · Director, Sangam Herbals — a family Ayurvedic house preparing remedies to classical texts, made in India

promise.title: We would rather lose a sale than overstate one.
promise.text: We exist to carry an unbroken 5,000-year tradition into homes around the world without diluting it — preparing every remedy to the classical Ayurvedic texts, from real herbs and cold-pressed oils, made at the source in India and answered for by name. So we show you exactly what is inside and how it is made, list every ingredient openly, and make no medical claims. Authenticity is our standard, not our exception.

checkout.ritual_title: While you wait — a small ritual
checkout.founder_note: Every order is answered for by name. — Dr. Praveen Rathi
checkout.email_copy_note: A copy of your order is on its way to your inbox.
success_tips: Begin tomorrow with a glass of warm water and a slow breath — the Ayurvedic way to wake the body gently.
Ayurveda is built from small, repeated habits. Choose one product, one moment a day, and let it become a ritual.
Warm a little oil between your palms before you apply it — warmth helps the herbs feel at home on the skin.
Evenings are for winding down: dim the lights, sip a warm herbal tea, and let the day settle.
Consistency over intensity — a gentle daily practice carries Ayurveda further than any quick fix.
